utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive planning documents that satisfy all six evaluation criteria. Both recommend White Oak with trade-off reasoning, address end-grain sealing as a distinct step, cover grain orientation for structural integrity, provide hardwood-specific tool guidance, include climate-relevant maintenance schedules, and identify outdoor-specific failure modes. The wo_skills output is actually slightly larger (14,977 bytes vs 12,895 bytes) and contains comparable technical depth. In practice, the outputs are qualitatively similar enough that no meaningful differentiation is observed across the evaluation criteria. If anything, wo_skills produced a marginally more detailed document by word count, suggesting the baseline agent in this case was already capable of producing expert-level content without the skill. Overall quality is similar, with a slight edge to wi_skills for more structured skill-backed reasoning, but wo_skills is not meaningfully inferior on any evaluated dimension.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive guides that passed all verifier checks and satisfied all six judge evaluation items. However, wi_skills produces a marginally superior guide: it is more precisely tailored to the specific koi relief scenario, uses a cleaner structured comparison table with more explicit reasoning, and the depth-layer plan and tool assignments are more granular and bench-ready. The wi_skills guide also reflects expert-level grain-direction thinking more explicitly (e.g., recommending 7/8–1 inch thickness and orienting grain along the 14-inch axis for structural reasons). The wo_skills guide is also strong — notably well-organized with a concise 'Recommended Exact Plan' prescription section — but its tool assignments and anti-pattern warnings are slightly less specific to the koi design's organic curves. Overall: wi_skills is more effective, though the gap is smaller than expected given that the wo_skills agent produced a genuinely expert-quality document through its own reasoning.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both agents successfully produced the output file. utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced comprehensive, professionally structured plans that pass all six evaluation criteria. The quality gap between the two runs is narrower than the test rationale predicted, because the wo_skills agent independently applied expert-level reasoning (end-grain sealing, grain orientation for 3D structural integrity, hardwood tool adjustments, climate-specific maintenance, outdoor failure modes) without requiring the skill. However, wi_skills shows marginally higher quality in specificity: it explicitly frames white oak vs. black locust trade-offs around the intermediate carver's tool set and fatigue risk (a nuanced workability argument), provides a cleaner decision matrix table, and its tool guidance is slightly more granular (specific gouge numbers mapped to specific animal body areas, bevel angle discussion). The wo_skills plan is also strong but uses black locust as primary — which is arguably the more durability-optimized but less carver-friendly choice — without as much explicit acknowledgment of the intermediate carver profile tradeoff. Overall, wi_skills is slightly more effective due to better integration of carver skill level into the species recommendation and more granular tool-technique specificity, but both outputs would be considered professional-grade deliverables.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced comprehensive, project-specific guides that technically pass all six evaluation criteria. Both score 6/6 on the binary pass/fail criteria, but wi_skills delivers substantially higher practical value to the beginner carver.
